backup.title: MongoDB Backups
backup.subtitle: Back up your MongoDB databases, restore across servers, or download a dump to your desktop.

fields.label: Label
fields.uriHint: mongodb:// or mongodb+srv:// — stored encrypted, never shown again.
fields.uriEditHint: Leave empty to keep the current URI.
fields.defaultDbHint: Optional — used when you don't specify one at backup time.
fields.targetDbHint: Which database to restore into (on the target connection).
fields.drop: Drop target collections before restoring (clean restore)

empty.connections: No connections yet. Add a MongoDB connection to start backing up.
empty.backups: No backups yet. Run a backup from the Connections tab.

dialog.connDesc: Register a MongoDB connection. The URI is encrypted at rest and never displayed again.
dialog.restoreDesc: Restore this backup into a target connection — same server or another.

confirm.deleteConnBody: This removes "{label}" and all of its backup records and stored artifacts. This cannot be undone.
confirm.deleteJobBody: This deletes the record and its stored artifact. This cannot be undone.
confirm.restoreBody: This will restore into database "{db}" on {target}. Drop mode: {drop}. This overwrites data on the target and cannot be undone.

warnings.restore: Restore overwrites data on the target database. Make sure you selected the correct target — this cannot be undone.

toast.backupStarted: Backup started — track its progress under Backups.
toast.restoreStarted: Restore started — track its progress under Backups.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
